classifications.phrase: inflation still some way above the midpoint of the 2–3 per cent target range
Broader data indicate continuing excess demand in the economy
elevated domestic cost pressures, for both labour and non-labour inputs
Conditions in the labour market eased further over the past month but remain tighter than…
Wages growth appears to have peaked but is still above the level that can be…
output growth has been subdued, and consumption per capita has been declining
The persistence of services price inflation is a key uncertainty
growth in unit labour costs has eased, it remains high
there is a risk that household consumption picks up more slowly than expected, resulting in…
Inflation is easing but has been doing so more slowly than previously expected and it…
remain vigilant to upside risks to inflation
The Board remains resolute in its determination to return inflation to target

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance by emphasizing persistent inflation above target, excess demand, elevated cost pressures, and upside risks, while acknowledging growth weakness and mixed data, resulting in a hold with vigilant monitoring.
